# Machine Learning Week presents

 HYBRID AI 2026

 San Francisco 

 May 5-6, 2026 | The Clift Royal Sonesta

[Register Now](https://machinelearningweek.com/registration/ "Register Now")[Agenda Live](https://machinelearningweek.com/agenda/ "Agenda Live")

![Image 5](https://machinelearningweek.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/29/2024/12/MLW-PAW-H2.png)

## 2026 KEYNOTES

[![Image 6](https://machinelearningweek.com/) Tuesday, May 5, 2026 ![Image 7](https://machinelearningweek.com/wp-content/themes/extramodular/projects/conference/assets/img/uk.png) Keynote: Predictive AI’s New Killer App: GenAI’s Reliability Layer](https://machinelearningweek.com/#)

Speaker/s:

[Dr. Eric Siegel,Machine Learning Week](https://machinelearningweek.com/speaker/dr-eric-siegel/)

[](https://machinelearningweek.com/#)

![Image 8](https://machinelearningweek.com/)

### Speaker/s:

[Dr. Eric Siegel](https://machinelearningweek.com/speaker/dr-eric-siegel/)

### Languages:

![Image 9](https://machinelearningweek.com/wp-content/themes/extramodular/projects/conference/assets/img/uk.png)

## Keynote: Predictive AI’s New Killer App: GenAI’s Reliability Layer

### Date:

Tuesday, May 5, 2026

### Time:

8:20 am

### Summary:

The most ideal way to soften the AI bubble’s looming detonation would be to boost AI’s realized value. How? A new reliability layer that tames large language models.

Enter predictive AI. LLM-based projects are usually too unreliable to move from pilot to production, but predictive AI can realize genAI’s bold, often overzealous promise of autonomy – or at least a great deal of it.

Join MLW conference founder Eric Siegel to learn why predictive AI is as crucial as ever in this “genAI world,” especially since it is positioned to solve genAI’s deadly reliability problem.

[![Image 10](https://machinelearningweek.com/) Tuesday, May 5, 2026 ![Image 11](https://machinelearningweek.com/wp-content/themes/extramodular/projects/conference/assets/img/uk.png) Keynote: The AI-Value Sweet Spot: Blending LLMs with Enterprise ML](https://machinelearningweek.com/#)

Speaker/s:

[Kirk Mettler,IBM](https://machinelearningweek.com/speaker/kirk-mettler/)

[](https://machinelearningweek.com/#)

![Image 12](https://machinelearningweek.com/)

### Speaker/s:

[Kirk Mettler](https://machinelearningweek.com/speaker/kirk-mettler/)

### Languages:

![Image 13](https://machinelearningweek.com/wp-content/themes/extramodular/projects/conference/assets/img/uk.png)

## Keynote: The AI-Value Sweet Spot: Blending LLMs with Enterprise ML

### Date:

Tuesday, May 5, 2026

### Time:

8:45 am

### Summary:

IBM Chief Data Scientist Kirk Mettler is one of those seasoned analytics veterans who don’t buy most of the AI hype– but still see a vital role for LLMs. His perspective plays out fortuitously as he dives into the weeds of advanced projects, even while he manages a large team of data scientists, data engineers, and other data professionals. “Today’s sky-rocketing investment into LLMs is just plain inappropriate,” Mettler says, “And yet, not leveraging LLMs to augment predictive AI projects would be just as inappropriate! We hit the sweet spot by blending LLMs with the time-honored tradition of enterprise machine learning.”

Conference Founder Eric Siegel will interview Mettler, digging in for what promises to be an extremely enlightening fireside keynote chat.

[![Image 14](https://machinelearningweek.com/) Tuesday, May 5, 2026 ![Image 15](https://machinelearningweek.com/wp-content/themes/extramodular/projects/conference/assets/img/uk.png) Keynote on Generative Architecture: An Imperative for AI Validation in the Architecture, Engineering, and Construction Industry](https://machinelearningweek.com/#)

Speaker/s:

[Julia Ling,X, the moonshot factory](https://machinelearningweek.com/speaker/julia-ling/)

[](https://machinelearningweek.com/#)

![Image 16](https://machinelearningweek.com/)

### Speaker/s:

[Julia Ling](https://machinelearningweek.com/speaker/julia-ling/)

### Languages:

![Image 17](https://machinelearningweek.com/wp-content/themes/extramodular/projects/conference/assets/img/uk.png)

## Keynote on Generative Architecture: An Imperative for AI Validation in the Architecture, Engineering, and Construction Industry

### Date:

Tuesday, May 5, 2026

### Time:

1:15 pm

### Summary:

The adoption of AI in technical fields is fueling a need for more rigorous, more modular, and more domain-specific validation frameworks. At Anori,[Alphabet X’s moonshot for housing and development](https://x.company/projects/anori/)[](https://x.company/projects/anori/), we are applying multimodal generative AI to make building and development dramatically faster, less costly and more efficient. The architecture, engineering, and construction industry suffers from entrenched inefficiency due to the highly constrained, highly regulated, and regionally-specific nature of building development that currently relies on numerous domain experts to sequentially flesh out a design.

Anori is creating a unified AI platform to change the paradigm and make it possible to design for many of these constraints simultaneously. Importantly, building design requires more than just beautiful images and eloquent descriptions: it requires engineering precision, regulatory compliance, and functional performance. In this setting, it is critical to have rigorous frameworks for evaluating model outputs at each step of the process, including both automated validation and human feedback. In this talk, we’ll discuss frameworks to bring transparency, diagnosability, and specificity to the model validation process.

[![Image 18](https://machinelearningweek.com/) Wednesday, May 6, 2026 ![Image 19](https://machinelearningweek.com/wp-content/themes/extramodular/projects/conference/assets/img/uk.png) Keynote: Context Engineering: How Machines Remember and Forget](https://machinelearningweek.com/#)

Speaker/s:

[Emre Okcular,OpenAI](https://machinelearningweek.com/speaker/emre-okcular/)

[](https://machinelearningweek.com/#)

![Image 20](https://machinelearningweek.com/)

### Speaker/s:

[Emre Okcular](https://machinelearningweek.com/speaker/emre-okcular/)

### Languages:

![Image 21](https://machinelearningweek.com/wp-content/themes/extramodular/projects/conference/assets/img/uk.png)

## Keynote: Context Engineering: How Machines Remember and Forget

### Date:

Wednesday, May 6, 2026

### Time:

8:45 am

### Summary:

The ‘magic moment’ for AI agents is hidden in the memory layer. Context Engineering is the art of shaping what an AI model knows at any moment by managing how information enters, persists, or fades from its working memory. In this session, we explore how machine learning systems “remember” through state objects, notes, and retrieval—and how they manage context using compression, selection, and context limits. We’ll walk through real-world agent patterns that balance personalization with privacy, performance, and relevance. Participants will learn practical techniques to design memory that feels intentional, evolving, and human-aware.

[![Image 22](https://machinelearningweek.com/) Wednesday, May 6, 2026 ![Image 23](https://machinelearningweek.com/wp-content/themes/extramodular/projects/conference/assets/img/uk.png) Keynote: AI Transformation Hinges on Very Particular Organizational Requirements](https://machinelearningweek.com/#)

Speaker/s:

[Jon Francis,State Farm](https://machinelearningweek.com/speaker/jon-francis/)

[](https://machinelearningweek.com/#)

![Image 24](https://machinelearningweek.com/)

### Speaker/s:

[Jon Francis](https://machinelearningweek.com/speaker/jon-francis/)

### Languages:

![Image 25](https://machinelearningweek.com/wp-content/themes/extramodular/projects/conference/assets/img/uk.png)

## Keynote: AI Transformation Hinges on Very Particular Organizational Requirements

### Date:

Wednesday, May 6, 2026

### Time:

1:15 pm

### Summary:

With an extensive record serving executive roles at the likes of Starbucks, GM, and State Farm, Jon Francis has learned what makes the difference between scalable AI systems and those that never get beyond pilots or even just ideation. “Here’s a hint,” he says. “At most companies, it has almost nothing to do with the math or technology, but instead hinges on culture, transformation, and change management.”

Join this fireside-chat keynote, where Machine Learning Week founder Eric Siegel will interview Francis and dive in.

## Why HYBRID AI 2026?

For 2026, Machine Learning Week returns to San Francisco, as**HYBRID AI 2026**. This is MLW’s 18th year, and perhaps its most important.

AI is on the cusp of greatness. The bad news is that positive returns are still few and far between – begging the question, when will AI finally achieve its greatness? The good news is that the final mile to more universally realized value is in sight.

**THE PROBLEM:**How can practitioners get genAI pilots to production – and get predictive AI from development to deployment – considering that the success rates are still extremely low?

### **THE SOLUTION:**

**1) Hybrid AI.**GenAI and Predictive AI are destined to marry because each is suited to address the other’s greatest limitations:_GenAI is often unreliable, while predictive AI is hard to use._

**2) A reliability layer to tame LLMs.**This layer must feature:

*   i) Continually expanding guardrails
*   ii) Strategically embedded humans in the loop –_indefinitely_
*   iii) Form-fitted customization for each project

**Why hybrid AI?**The _reliability layer_ demands a strategic hybridization of methods – such as predictive AI and genAI – as well as the strategic embedding of humans-in-the-loop (human/machine collaboration is also sometimes called “hybrid AI”).

The most ideal way to soften the AI bubble’s looming detonation would be to boost AI’s realized value. To this end, developing a reliability layer is a critical, emerging discipline. It’s vital for establishing system robustness that would make AI pilots ready for production. And it’s a fruitful way to test the very limits of LLMs, exploring and expanding the feasibility of industry’s ever-increasing AI ambitions.

Come to**HYBRID AI 2026**to turn AI’s potential into realized value– by discovering best practices that make AI products robust and deployment-worthy.

For more insight:

[Hybrid AI: Industry Event Signals Emerging Hot Trend](https://www.forbes.com/sites/ericsiegel/2026/02/09/hybrid-ai-industry-event-signals-emerging-hot-trend/)

[Predictive AI Thrives, Despite GenAI Stealing The Spotlight](https://www.forbes.com/sites/ericsiegel/2026/02/11/predictive-ai-thrives-despite-genai-stealing-the-spotlight/)

## Need more information? / FAQS

*    The history of Machine Learning Week  Machine Learning Week evolved from the [Predictive Analytics World (PAW) conferences, which began in 2009](https://www.predictiveanalyticsworld.com/machinelearningtimes/a-brief-history-of-paw-on-its-10-year-anniversary/9936/), running in multiple cities in the US and Europe each year. From 2018, in response to vendor and attendee requests to have one place they could meet everybody, various vertical conferences (PAW Business, PAW Industry 4.0, PAW Financial, PAW Healthcare), were brought together in one mega-event. This was met with an overwhelmingly positive reception from all participants. Deep Learning World was also launched as part of the family in 2018 and PAW Climate (which runs virtually) in 2021. All are now together as Machine Learning Week. In 2026 ML Week is running HYBRID AI in San Francisco, back where it all started. 
*    What is predictive AI?  Predictive AI (aka predictive analytics) is the application of enterprise machine learning to improve operations, such as optimizing marketing campaigns and website behavior to increase customer responses, conversions and clicks, and to decrease churn. Each customer’s predictive score informs actions to be taken with that customer — analytics just doesn’t get more actionable than that. 
*    Is predictive AI different from forecasting?  Machine Learning Week events often include select sessions on forecasting since it is a closely related area, and, in some cases, predictive AI is used as a component to build a forecast model.

However, predictive AI is something else entirely, going beyond standard forecasting by producing a predictive score for each customer or other organizational element. In contrast, forecasting provides overall aggregate estimates, such as the total number of purchases next quarter. For example, forecasting might estimate the total number of ice cream cones to be purchased in a certain region, while predictive AI tells you which individual customers are likely to buy an ice cream cone. 
*    Is this a “data science” conference?  Yes. Predictive analytics is a form of data science. Moreover, it is the most actionable form. A predictive model generates a predictive score for each individual, which in turn directly informs decisions for that individual, e.g., whether to contact, extend a retention offer, approve for credit, investigate for fraud, or apply a certain medical treatment. Rather than solely providing insights, predictive analytics directly drives or informs millions of operational decisions. 
*    Is this a “big data” conference?  Yes. Predictive AI is a key method to truly leverage big data. At the center of the big data revolution is prediction. The whole point of data is to learn from it to predict. What is the value, the function, the purpose? Predictions drive and render more effective the millions of organizational operational decisions taken every day.  

*    Is this an AI conference?  Yes. Artificial intelligence (AI) is a broadterm with many possible definitions—but by any definition, it always includes machine learning (predictive modeling) as an example of AI technology/capabilities.  
*    Is Machine Learning Week run by a software vendor?  No. Machine Learning Week provides a balanced view of predictive AI methods and tools across software vendors and solution providers.  
*    Is Machine Learning Week a research conference?  No. Machine Learning Week is focused on today’s commercial deployment of predictive AI, rather than academic or R&D activities. Separately, there are a number of research-oriented conferences; in predictive AI’s commercial application, we are essentially standing on the shoulders of those giants known as researchers.  
*    Are you considering new speakers for Machine Learning Week?  For speaker information and proposal submissions, [click here](https://machinelearningweek.com/call-for-speakers/).

### AgentCon San Francisco: Call for Speakers

AgentCon San Francisco is a developer-focused AI agents conference in Silicon Valley on May 4, 2026, featuring talks, hands-on workshops, and live demos. Learn how to build and integrate AI agents in real-world projects, with two tracks (Technical Sessions and Hands-on Workshops), plus networking.
What to expect:
- An immersive, one-day conference dedicated to practical AI agent development, aimed at developers, data scientists, researchers, and startup builders.
- Two tracks:
- Technical Sessions: 25–45 minute talks exploring architecture, implementation, and case studies of AI agents.
- Hands-on Workshop Track: 60–90 minute interactive sessions for deeper coding and live experimentation.
- A keynote and dedicated community networking sessions to connect with peers, mentors, and potential collaborators.
Key topics you’ll see and explore:
- Building AI Agents using frameworks and toolchains (for example, Semantic Kernel, LangChain, Autogen, CrewAI) and how they fit into real product workflows
- Integrating AI Agents into products, services, and business processes
- Multi-agent collaboration, orchestration patterns, and system design for scalable autonomous agents
- Prompt engineering, LLM optimization, evaluation, and usability considerations
- Responsible AI, ethics, governance, and security considerations for agent-based systems
- Developer tooling, SDKs, cloud integrations, observability, and monitoring for AI agents
- Hands-on demos, tutorials, and live coding to translate theory into runnable prototypes
Audience and goals:
- Targeted at developers, engineers, data scientists, architects, startup founders, researchers, and students who want practical, hands-on experience with agent-powered systems.
- Sessions should be educational, original, and aligned with the community-driven focus of AgentCon.
- Submissions welcome from seasoned professionals and first-time speakers alike.
Call for speakers details:
- Call opens at 12:00 AM and closes at 11:59 PM, with a submission window through March 15, 2026.
- Location: Computer Museum, Silicon Valley, San Francisco, California, United States.
- Website: https://globalai.community/chapters/san-francisco/events/agentcon-san-francisco/
- The event is part of the Global AI Community’s AgentCon series, inviting you to contribute sessions that educate, inspire, and empower developers building with AI agents.
How to participate:
- Visit the Event page and submit your talk proposal.
- Login or create an accou

### AgentCon San Francisco

AgentCon San Francisco is a one-day AI agents conference for developers, part of the AI Agents World Tour, offering hands-on demos, design talks, and deployment insights.
From San Francisco to Singapore, this event brings together leading engineers, researchers, and creators to explore the cutting edge of AI agent design, deployment, and integration. Attendees will dive into practical knowledge and real-world use cases for building autonomous assistants, intelligent agents, and developer tools that leverage AI to automate tasks, orchestrate workflows, and extend software capabilities. Whether you’re prototyping new agent architectures, refining deployment pipelines, or exploring no-code/low-code paths to AI agents, you’ll leave with actionable ideas and fresh perspectives.
What to expect:
- Keynotes and technical talks focused on AI agent design, deployment strategies, and system integration
- Hands-on demos showcasing the end-to-end lifecycle of AI agents, from concept to production
- Real-world case studies illustrating how developers instrument, test, and scale agent-based solutions
- Interactive Q&A and collaboration opportunities with practitioners building the next generation of intelligent tools
- Networking with peers across engineering, research, and product disciplines
Who should attend:
- AI/ML engineers, researchers, and data scientists
- Software developers and architects exploring agent-based architectures
- Founders and builders crafting AI-assisted developer tools or autonomous systems
- No-code/low-code practitioners interested in agent-enabled automation
Format and logistics:
- Date and time: May 4, 2026, 9:00 AM local time (San Francisco)
- In-person conference format with talks, demos, and networking sessions
- Registration portal available at the official event page: https://globalai.community/chapters/san-francisco/events/agentcon-san-francisco/
- Learn more on the Meetup page: https://www.meetup.com/graphdb-sf/events/313520551
Why this matters: AI agents are reshaping how apps automate tasks, compose services, and interact with users. AgentCon San Francisco provides a focused, practitioner-led environment to share patterns, tooling choices, and deployment experiences that accelerate building reliable, scalable agent-powered software.
Pro-tip: Bring your questions about architecture trade-offs, testing AI agents in production, and integrating agents with no-code platforms. If you’re exploring automation pathways or curious how t

![AI Council 2026 — San Francisco](https://secure.meetupstatic.com/photos/event/6/8/f/e/600_533486878.jpeg)

### AI Council 2026 — San Francisco

AI Council 2026 brings the frontier of artificial intelligence to [San Francisco](https://www.createwith.com/cities/san-francisco) for three intensive days of technical learning, hands-on experimentation, and deep networking. Running May 12-14, 2026 at the Marriott Marquis in SOMA, this vendor-neutral conference is designed for engineers, researchers, and builders who are actively shaping the future of AI.
## What Makes AI Council Different
AI Council stands apart with its "No BS Guarantee" — every session is vendor-neutral, technically rigorous, and presented by practitioners doing real implementation work at companies like Anthropic, OpenAI, Meta, and Databricks. This isn't a marketing showcase; it's a community-driven event focused on sharing production learnings, architectural decisions, and hard-won insights from teams building AI systems at scale.
## Conference Structure
The event features \*\*60+ talks\*\* distributed across \*\*10 specialized tracks\*\*: Inference Systems, AI Engineering, AI Security & Safety, Agent Infrastructure, Coding Agents & Autonomous Dev, Model Systems, Data Engineering & Databases, Applied AI, Analytics & Data Science, and Lightning Talks. This structure allows attendees to dive deep into their areas of focus while exposing them to adjacent domains that increasingly intersect in production AI deployments.
Beyond the main stage, AI Council includes \*\*hands-on workshops\*\* led by tool creators, giving you direct experience with cutting-edge products and frameworks. After each talk, \*\*speaker office hours\*\* provide rare 1:1 access to presenters — an opportunity to explore technical details, discuss implementation challenges, or get feedback on your own projects.
## Who Should Attend
This conference is ideal for software engineers building AI-powered applications, data scientists deploying models to production, ML engineers optimizing inference systems, platform engineers supporting AI infrastructure, and technical leaders evaluating AI strategies. The content assumes technical familiarity and focuses on real-world implementation rather than high-level theory.
## Networking & Community
AI Council creates extensive opportunities for meaningful connections through conference happy hours and community-hosted after-hours events throughout the week. The single-track-within-themes format and speaker accessibility foster deeper conversations than typical large-scale conferences.
As a member of NYC Data Engineering & Science meetups,

![AI Dev Showcase Night SF - May Edition](https://www.aicamp.ai/img/w2026051317b.png)

### AI Dev Showcase Night SF - May Edition

## What to Expect
Designed for developers and platform teams navigating the challenging transition from AI prototypes to production systems, this technical meetup examines why most AI workflows fail to scale and what actually works in real-world deployments. While experiments and POCs may show promise, production environments demand robust workflows, governance, and trust mechanisms that many teams struggle to implement.
## Featured Tech Talk
\*\*From AI Experiments to Production: How Teams Actually Scale AI Workflows\*\* will be presented by Rob Whiteley from Coder. This deep-dive session addresses the critical pain points facing development teams: experiments that work locally but break in production, AI-generated code that accelerates development but disrupts established workflows, autonomous agents that produce output teams can't trust or scale, and governance frameworks added too late that throttle velocity.
The talk focuses on redesigning development workflows to work harmoniously with AI rather than simply layering new tools onto existing processes. You'll explore practical approaches to structuring workflows, managing autonomous agents, and introducing appropriate controls that maintain developer productivity. This isn't a tool comparison or hype session — it's a technical examination of production realities backed by real-world implementation experiences.
## Event Format
The evening begins with registration and networking over food (5:30-6:00pm), followed by community updates and welcomes (6:00-6:15pm). The main tech talk and Q&A session runs from 6:15-7:30pm, providing ample time for deep technical discussion. The event concludes with an open discussion and networking mixer (7:30-8:30pm), giving you extended opportunities to connect with speakers and fellow developers tackling similar challenges.
## Who Should Attend
This showcase is built for technical audiences — developers, ML engineers, platform engineers, DevOps professionals, and engineering leaders responsible for moving AI capabilities from experimentation to production. Whether you're struggling with agent reliability, workflow redesign, governance implementation, or production scaling challenges, you'll find actionable insights and peer connections.
## Community & Networking
Co-hosted by AICamp and Workato, this series brings together two major developer communities for high-impact technical networking. AICamp's global community of 500,000+ AI developers combines with Workato's en

![AWS Partner Showcase (SF) - Accelerate Agentic AI in Production](https://www.aicamp.ai/img/w2026051817b.png)

### AWS Partner Showcase (SF) - Accelerate Agentic AI in Production

Join AWS and leading industry partners at the AWS Loft in [San Francisco](https://www.createwith.com/cities/san-francisco) for an in-depth exploration of production-ready agentic AI systems. This fourth edition of the AWS Agentic AI Partner Showcase brings together technical experts from AWS, Coder, Mistral AI, Workato, Arize AI, and Moody's to demonstrate the complete development lifecycle—from initial code through deployment, monitoring, and validation.
## What Makes This Event Unique
This isn't just another AI talk series. The showcase features live demonstrations of the complete development stack required to build, deploy, and maintain agentic AI systems in production environments. You'll see real implementations, not theoretical concepts, with practical insights into AWS integration patterns and production deployment strategies.
The event combines main-stage technical presentations with interactive partner booths, allowing you to engage directly with solution providers throughout the evening. This dual format ensures you can both learn from expert demonstrations and dive deep into specific technical questions with the teams building these solutions.
## Learning Outcomes
Attendees will gain comprehensive understanding of \*\*production-ready agentic AI development\*\*, seeing the entire stack from coding environments through deployment and monitoring infrastructure. The technical implementation strategies session reveals how AWS and partners approach real-world deployments, complete with integration patterns you can apply immediately.
The expert Q&A sessions at dedicated booth stations provide direct access to technical leaders, while networking opportunities connect you with fellow developers and practitioners actively building agentic AI applications. Director of Partner Technology at AWS, Rohan Karmarkar, leads the proceedings alongside partner representatives.
## Event Format
The four-hour programme begins with check-in, food, and booth exploration from 4:30-5:30 PM, allowing early networking. The main technical content runs 5:30-7:45 PM with kick-off, tech talks, and live demonstrations. The final session (7:45-8:30 PM) offers extended networking and booth Q&A, perfect for detailed technical discussions.
This event targets developers, AI practitioners, and technical leaders working with or planning to implement agentic AI systems. Whether you're evaluating AWS infrastructure for AI deployment, seeking production-ready solutions, or wanting to

![AI Agent Security Summit: San Francisco](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/bqvkdjz2/production/fd7a88d26d2ab85896da7567618a14631c8d3912-1920x1080.png)

### AI Agent Security Summit: San Francisco

The AI revolution is entering a critical new phase — and security leaders are gathering in [San Francisco](/cities/san-francisco) to address it head-on. Following October 2025's landmark summit, Zenity's AI Agent Security Summit returns to the Commonwealth Club on May 27, 2026, at a watershed moment when the agentic attack surface has fundamentally transformed.
## Why This Summit Matters Now
The last six months have brought seismic shifts to AI agent security. The industry now has its first globally peer-reviewed security framework with the \*\*OWASP Top 10 for Agentic Applications\*\*, providing structured guidance for protecting AI agents. Meanwhile, [OpenClaw](/tools/openclaw) — an open-source agent assistant that rapidly gained over 250,000 GitHub stars — has introduced critical new vulnerabilities, including indirect prompt injection risks that make it susceptible to backdoors. The emergence of agentic browsers like [Perplexity](/tools/perplexity)'s Comet has further expanded the risk surface, demonstrating how attackers can weaponize these tools to compromise systems like 1Password vaults.
## What You'll Explore
This summit tackles the fundamental question: what's changed in 2026, what's noise, and what's next? Security professionals will unpack the rapidly evolving landscape of shadow AI adoption, examine emergent frameworks, and dissect real-world vulnerabilities in widely-adopted open-source tools. The event brings together the conversation around purpose-built security for an era where AI agents operate with increasing autonomy and access.
## Who Should Attend
This gathering is essential for anyone working at the intersection of AI agents and security — from security leaders at disruptive startups to established enterprises and hyperscalers. Enterprise agentic development is moving at breakneck pace, and this community aims to navigate the adoption journey smoothly and securely. You'll network with experts who understand the unique challenges of securing autonomous AI systems, share experiences from your own implementation journey, and contribute your perspective to guide peers during this critical inflection point.
The summit builds on content from the October 2025 event (available on-demand) while addressing the staggering new developments that have emerged since. Whether you're grappling with securing existing agent deployments or planning your organisation's agentic strategy, you'll leave with actionable frameworks and connections to help

# AI Makers Global Mixer - Meet AI Tech Professionals

![Photo of the user Kev P.](https://secure.meetupstatic.com/photos/member/8/4/4/6/thumb_325413862.jpeg?w=64)

Hosted by Kev P.

![Photo of Global AI Makers • SF • AI CEOs, Founders, Makers group](https://secure.meetupstatic.com/photos/event/1/8/0/d/event_533706157.jpeg?w=3840)

### Global AI Makers • SF • AI CEOs, Founders, Makers

## Details

**AI Makers Global Mixer — The Monthly Gathering for Everyone Building with AI**

Every month, the people building the most interesting things in AI get together in one room.

Founders who just shipped their first AI agent. Engineers integrating LLMs into products that exist. Operators who automated something at work last week and cannot stop thinking about what else is possible.

All of them. One room. One hour. Online. Free.

This is the **AI Makers Global Mixer.**

There are no keynotes. No pitch decks. No panels of people you have already heard from. Just the community — builders and founders and professionals who are working with AI right now — showing up to meet each other, share what they are making, and find the people they did not know they needed to meet.

**What Happens**  
The event opens with a short message. After that the room opens for open conversation, networking, and connection.

If you work in AI, build with AI, invest in AI, write about AI, or are figuring out how AI changes your industry — this event was built for you.

Founders and CEOs bring their latest builds and find early users. Engineers show experiments and find collaborators. Investors watch what is being shipped before it makes the news. Journalists find the real story. Students find the people they want to work with. Everyone finds someone worth knowing.

Register free.

Bring one person who should be in this room.

**When:** Every third Thursday of the month, 11:00 AM CST / 9:00 AM PT / 12:00 PM ET / 5:00 PM London

**Where:** Live online.

**Format:** 5 minute introductions, open networking in chat and connections

**Submit your wishes for introductions:** [aitodone.com/join](https://aitodone.com/join)

**Submit your apps:** [aitodone.com/makers/submit](http://aitodone.com/makers/submit)

**The AI Makers Directory:** Every project shared at every event, permanent and searchable: [aitodone.com](http://aitodone.com)

**Cost:** Free. Always.

**About the Host**  
Kev has spent 14 years helping make ideas worth joining. Across platforms reaching 100M+ users (PagerDuty, Liquibase, and others), Kev has built communities, spoken globally, and helped technology earn trust rather than just attention. You can learn more at the links below:  
<https://www.linkedin.com/in/hellokev/>  
<https://aitodone.com>

![AI DevSummit 2026](/e/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimg.evbuc.com%2Fhttps%253A%252F%252Fcdn.evbuc.com%252Fimages%252F1180344168%252F30266753513%252F1%252Foriginal.20260320-200031%3Fcrop%3Dfocalpoint%26fit%3Dcrop%26w%3D600%26auto%3Dformat%252Ccompress%26q%3D75%26sharp%3D10%26fp-x%3D0.5%26fp-y%3D0.5%26s%3De867b4b9769865d49422393370feb4fb&w=1880&q=75)

# AI DevSummit 2026

AI DevSummit 2026 is the premier conference for engineers and data teams shipping real-world AI

## ****AI DevSummit 2026****

## ****May 27-28, 2026 | South San Francisco Center - San Francisco, CA****

AI DevSummit brings together AI/ML engineers, data scientists, and technical leads for two days of hands-on sessions, case studies, and conversations focused on production-scale systems. From managing training pipelines and inference performance to MLOps, GenAI, and LLM architecture, the focus is on what works at scale—no fluff, no hype. Whether you're fine-tuning models, deploying edge AI, or integrating AI into enterprise platforms, AI DevSummit is where teams share what’s actually shipping and why it matters. If you’re building AI systems, this is your technical community.

With a fully immersive experience, we're bringing you: 6+ tracks, 70+ speakers, technical keynotes, a live Expo with 30+ technologies & tools, product demos, and more.

AI DevSummit 2026 is the premier conference for engineers and data teams shipping real-world AI

## ****AI DevSummit 2026****

## ****May 27-28, 2026 | South San Francisco Center - San Francisco, CA****

AI DevSummit brings together AI/ML engineers, data scientists, and technical leads for two days of hands-on sessions, case studies, and conversations focused on production-scale systems. From managing training pipelines and inference performance to MLOps, GenAI, and LLM architecture, the focus is on what works at scale—no fluff, no hype. Whether you're fine-tuning models, deploying edge AI, or integrating AI into enterprise platforms, AI DevSummit is where teams share what’s actually shipping and why it matters. If you’re building AI systems, this is your technical community.

With a fully immersive experience, we're bringing you: 6+ tracks, 70+ speakers, technical keynotes, a live Expo with 30+ technologies & tools, product demos, and more.

OVERVIEW AGENDA:

In-Person:

**AI DevSummit is produced and owned by DevNetwork, the world's developer event community and producer of leading conferences for the developer, engineering & IT industries.**

**By registering for this event, you are opting into DevNetwork email updates and one-time email-based promotions from top-level AI DevSummit sponsors including exclusive invitations to AI DevSummit parties and partner events. These updates will notify you about AI DevSummit news, as well as announcements about DevNetwork events and services. You can opt out of DevNetwork email updates at any time.**

**Refund Policy: Due to limited availability, DevNetwork cannot offer refunds once your tickets are purchased. If you cannot make the event due to unforeseen circumstances, you may transfer your ticket(s), or we will give you a credit towards a future conference of equal or lesser value. Thank you.**

## Good to know

#### SHAPING THE FUTURE OF AI

*   Sept 29 - Oct 1
*   SAN FRANCISCO

## GET $1,925 OFF

[Register Now](https://aiconference.com/#tickets)

## Price Increase

08 Days

15 Hours

19 Minutes

48 Seconds

 SPEAKERS 

Meet the **first confirmed of 120+ speakers** for The AI Conference 2026. Additional speakers, presentations, and features will be announced weekly.

![Image 3](https://aiconference.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/Peter-Norvig.jpg)

###### Peter Norvig

Distinguished Education Fellow

![Image 4](https://aiconference.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/HAI.png)

Peter Norvig is a Distinguished Education Fellow at Stanford’s Human-Centered Artificial Intelligence Institute and formerly directed research at Google and NASA’s Ames Research Center.

[Wikipedia-w](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Peter_Norvig)[Globe](https://www.norvig.com/)[Linkedin](https://www.linkedin.com/in/pnorvig)

![Image 5](https://aiconference.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/Emmanuel-Ameisen.jpg)

###### Emmanuel Ameisen

Interpretability Researcher

![Image 6](https://aiconference.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/Anthropic.png)

Emmanuel Ameisen is an interpretability researcher at Anthropic, where he works on understanding how large language models work internally.

[Globe](https://www.mlpowered.com/)[Linkedin](https://www.linkedin.com/in/ameisen)[X-twitter](https://x.com/mlpowered)

![Image 7](https://aiconference.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/Sarah-Chieng.jpg)

###### Sarah Chieng

Head of Developer Experience

![Image 8](https://aiconference.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/cerebras.png)

Sarah Chieng leads Developer Experience at Cerebras, focusing on AI hardware and developer ecosystems, and founded Cafe Compute, a community for AI builders and researchers.

[Globe](https://sarahchieng.com/)[Linkedin](https://www.linkedin.com/in/sarah-chieng-888595139)[X-twitter](https://x.com/SarahChieng)

![Image 9](https://aiconference.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/Ionnis-Antonoglu.jpg)

###### Ioannis Antonoglou

Co-founder and CTO

![Image 10](https://aiconference.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/Reflection.png)

Ioannis Antonoglou is a founding engineer at Google DeepMind and core architect behind AlphaGo, AlphaZero, and MuZero, and now serves as co‑founder and CTO of Reflection AI.

[Globe](https://reflection.ai/)[Linkedin](https://www.linkedin.com/in/ioannis-alexandros-antonoglou-45393253)

![Image 11](https://aiconference.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/Jaclyn-Konzelmann.jpg)

###### Jaclyn Konzelmann

Product Director

![Image 12](https://aiconference.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/Google-Labs.png)

**The Agentic Shift:** How to Build Products When AI Can Actually Do Things

_Leads AI‑first products at Google Labs, including Gemini API and Google AI Studio._

[Globe](https://jaclynkonzelmann.com/)[Linkedin](https://www.linkedin.com/in/jaclynkonzelmann)[X-twitter](https://x.com/jacalulu)

![Image 13](https://aiconference.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/Marco-Paglia.jpg)

###### Marco Paglia

President - Product & Engineering

![Image 14](https://aiconference.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/musixmatch-1.png)

**Lyrics:** The Language Model of Music

_Leads product and engineering at Musixmatch, the global music data company._

[Linkedin](https://www.linkedin.com/in/marcopaglia)[Youtube](https://www.youtube.com/@musixmatch)[Medium](https://medium.com/@paglia_marco)

![Image 15](https://aiconference.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/Jon-Pappas.jpg)

###### Jon Pappas

Sr. Dir. Technical Product Management

![Image 16](https://aiconference.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/upwork-1.png)

**Beyond the Benchmark:** Building for the Complexity of Real Marketplaces

Leads AI‑powered talent search, matching, and deployment at Upwork.

[Linkedin](https://www.linkedin.com/in/mindfulent)[Github](https://github.com/mindfulent)

![Image 17](https://aiconference.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/Yogiraj-Awati.jpg)

###### Yogiraj Awati

Engineering Manager

![Image 18](https://aiconference.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/instacart.png)

**Deploying LLMs in Production to enhance grocery shopping**

Leads large-scale AI and RAG platforms powering Instacart’s grocery experience.

[Linkedin](https://www.linkedin.com/in/yogirajawati)[Github](https://github.com/yogiraj07)

![Image 19](https://aiconference.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/Ryan-Lopopolo.jpg)

###### Ryan Lopopolo

Member of Technical Staff

![Image 20](https://aiconference.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/OpenAi-1.png)

**Harness Engineering:** How to Build Software When Humans Steer and Agents Execute

Builds agent‑first development workflows at OpenAI, authoring the Harness Engineering framework.

[Globe](https://hyperbo.la/)[Linkedin](https://www.linkedin.com/in/ryanlopopolo/)[Github](https://github.com/lopopolo)[X-twitter](https://x.com/_lopopolo)

![Image 21](https://aiconference.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/Pallavi-Kinnera.jpg)

###### Pallavi Kinnera

Chip Designer

![Image 22](https://aiconference.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/Meta.png)

**One of the Most Complex Thing Humans Build:** How AI Is Finally Cracking Chip Design

Leads AI‑driven automation for advanced‑node chip design at Meta Reality Labs.

[Linkedin](https://www.linkedin.com/in/pallavi-kinnera)

![Image 23](https://aiconference.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/Shuen-Mei.jpg)

###### Shuen Mei

VP, Director of Software and Data Engineering

![Image 24](https://aiconference.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/Harbor-Capital-Advisors.png)

**Act–Verify–Refine:** The Future of Adaptive Investment Agents

Leads AI, data, and software teams building Harbor Capital Advisors’ data, cloud, and integration platforms across financial services.

[Globe](https://www.harborcapital.com/)[Linkedin](https://www.linkedin.com/in/shuen-mei-39697a14)

![Image 25](https://aiconference.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/Pratyusha-Singaraju.jpg)

###### Pratyusha Singaraju

Senior Software Engineer

![Image 26](https://aiconference.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/Netflix.png)

**The Content Machine:** Orchestrating Content Workflows at Netflix Scale

Designs and builds AI‑powered backend services driving content understanding for Netflix search and discovery.

[Linkedin](https://www.linkedin.com/in/pratyusha-singaraju)

![Image 27](https://aiconference.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/Julia-Diament.jpg)

###### Julia Diament

Principal AI Architect, Office of the CTO

![Image 28](https://aiconference.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/Microsoft.png)

**Cyber Offense Hits Its Moore’s Law Moment** – when compute gets cheap, persistence ‎becomes the exploit.‎

Brings 18+ years of AI and security expertise to Microsoft’s Office of the CTO, focused on adversarial threats and secure‑by‑design patterns.

[Linkedin](https://www.linkedin.com/in/julia-diament)

![Image 29](https://aiconference.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/Divya-Mahajan.jpg)

###### Divya Mahajan

Software Engineer

![Image 30](https://aiconference.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/amazon.png)

**8 Ways Production Agents Fail Silently** (And the Detection Patterns That Catch Them)

Builds production‑grade agentic AI and LLM systems at Alexa scale, leading evaluation and multi‑turn workflows for Alexa+ users.

[Linkedin](https://www.linkedin.com/in/dm-divyamahajan/)

![Image 31](https://aiconference.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/Ruiyang-Wang.jpg)

###### Ruiyang Wang

Member of Technical Staff

![Image 32](https://aiconference.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/Anthropic.png)

**Safe PDF Processing at Scale:** A Sandbox-Rasterize-OCR Architecture

Builds infrastructure and data systems behind Anthropic’s large language models, previously at Anyscale and Google Cloud.

[Linkedin](https://www.linkedin.com/in/ruiyang-wang)[Github](https://github.com/rynewang)

![Image 33](https://aiconference.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/Vidya-Meenakshi.jpg)

###### Vidya Meenakshi

Senior Software Engineer,

![Image 34](https://aiconference.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/INTUIT.png)

**When Your AI Agent’s Conversation Breaks Forever:** Production Lessons from Building an Agent in a Multi-Agent System

Builds backend experimentation platforms at Intuit, including the Experimentation Agent and MCP server that scale data‑driven decisions.

[Linkedin](https://www.linkedin.com/in/vidya-meenakshi-09)

[SEE ALL SPEAKERS](https://aiconference.com/speakers/)

 Register 

## Day ZERØ

##### Sept 29

**Day ZERØ** is a high-signal, small-room kickoff where senior builders and leaders spend a full day moving between focused workshops and a live AI Hack Day before the main conference. The AI Conference full experience starts here. **Includes full conference pass.**

###### [FULL Day ZERØ Info](https://aiconference.com/dz/)

[Focused Workshops - Limited Capacity](https://aiconference.com/)

Capped at 300 people, this where AI ideas go from concept to usable tech in real-time. It includes **technical** or **leadership** tracks (6 sessions, 90 min each), deeper discussions, and direct access to speakers and peers.

**Technical Track**

Deep-dive, hands-on sessions for engineers and builders covering model development, RAG, infra, evaluation, agents, and real-world deployment patterns.

**Leadership Track**

Strategy-focused sessions for founders and executives on AI roadmaps, product strategy, governance, organizational change, and safely scaling AI in production.

[Guaranteed Hack Day Access](https://aiconference.com/)

![Image 35](https://aiconference.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/Hack-Day-26.png)Observe or participate in a live AI build environment. See frontier ideas move from concept to working systems in real time.

[VIP Evening Dinner & Reception](https://aiconference.com/)

An intimate gathering with fellow operators and speakers designed for meaningful conversations before the larger conference begins.

[Full Conference Access + Early Badge Pickup](https://aiconference.com/)

![Image 36](https://aiconference.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/07/AI-Conference_Icon.svg)Start early, avoid lines, and move seamlessly into the two-day conference with complete access to all programming.

[Attendee Only Perks](https://aiconference.com/)

Access to all **AI Week** events, including **Party in the Park**, **Ignite Talks**, and exclusive attendee networking events.

Be where AI ideas go from concept to usable tech in real-time.This is ground zero for where execution meets opportunity.

## GET $1,925 OFF

[Register Now](https://aiconference.com/#elementor-action%3Aaction%3Dpopup%3Aopen%26settings%3DeyJpZCI6IjI5NTI2IiwidG9nZ2xlIjpmYWxzZX0%3D)

## Price Increase

08 Days

15 Hours

19 Minutes

48 Seconds

This will sell out quickly

## Full Conference

##### Sept 30 - Oct 1

**Day 1 and 2** of The AI Conference brings together 5,500+ builders, researchers, and leaders for two full days of five-track programming, main-stage keynotes, the Startup Showdown, and an expanded Innovation Hub, focused on applied AI from research to production.​​

###### Full two Day Conference

[Five Curated Tracks](https://aiconference.com/)

Dedicated programming spanning applied AI, technical depth, infrastructure, product, and leadership—so you can focus on the conversations most relevant to your work.

[120 + Speakers](https://aiconference.com/)

Founders, engineers, researchers, and operators sharing real implementation stories, technical insight, and lessons from scaling AI in production.

See the currently announced speakers **[here](https://aiconference.com/#speakers)**

[Startup Showdown](https://aiconference.com/)

Emerging AI companies take the stage to present live. Competing, demonstrating, and connecting with investors, partners, and the broader ecosystem.

[The Innovation Hub](https://aiconference.com/)

An expanded showcase of emerging tools, platforms, and startups shaping the next generation of AI.

[Attendee Only Perks](https://aiconference.com/)

Access to all **AI Week** events, including **Party in the Park**, **Ignite Talks**, and exclusive attendee networking events.

Attendees can expect concrete architectures, practical lessons from teams shipping AI today.

## GET $1,925 OFF

[Register Now](https://aiconference.com/#elementor-action%3Aaction%3Dpopup%3Aopen%26settings%3DeyJpZCI6IjI5NTI2IiwidG9nZ2xlIjpmYWxzZX0%3D)

## Price Increase

08 Days

19 Minutes

48 Seconds

 Team Tickets 

## Team Up and Save

**Equip your team for what’s next in AI.** Team Tickets are our best value, giving your group two focused days at The AI Conference 2026 to learn from real systems at our lowest cost per person.

**This two-day Team Ticket experience helps your team:**
*   Spot where AI can cut costs or open new revenue
*   Turn conference time into a concrete roadmap your team owns
*   Give your team a clear view of the AI landscape, from infrastructure to applications
*   Leave with a shortlist of AI tools, patterns, and partners to act on

 Party in the Park 

## Networking Event

**All registered attendees** get an invite to the AI industry’s most anticipated private after-hours event.

**Party in the Park** is an open-air celebration where ideas meet vibes and the AI community comes to life under the stars.

**This exclusive event will feature:**

*   Live music that keeps the energy high
*   Ice-cold drinks and good food
*   Big giveaways
*   A surprise announcement you’ll want to be there for

[![Image 38](https://aiconference.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/Party-in-the-Park.jpg)](https://aiconference.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/Party-in-the-Park.jpg)

 TRACKS 

## AI Frontiers

**Explore** governance, ethics, societal impact, and frontier research—multimodal, agentic, and world models included.

Perfect for researchers, policy leaders, and senior strategists.

## AI Builders

**Learn how to ship** agentic and multi-LLM systems with the right toolchains, fine-tuning, retrieval, and scaling patterns.

Perfect for software engineers, architects, and tech leads.

## The AI Stack

**Dive deep** into model design and training, distributed inference, data and evaluations, accelerators, and MLOps.

Perfect for ML researchers, ML/AI engineers, and infrastructure teams.

## Applied AI

**See how real deployments work**: workflow automation, edge/on-device intelligence, rigorous evaluation, and case studies.

Perfect for product managers, applied scientists, and solutions/operations leaders.

## AI Strategy

**Lead enterprise adoption** with governance, risk management, capability building, ROI frameworks, and culture change.

Perfect for executives, strategists, and transformation owners.

 Testimonials 

## Past Attendees

**Real voices from last year’s crowd.** Attendees talk candidly about the sessions, speakers, and community that made The AI Conference valuable, and why they recommend it to people serious about AI.

These unscripted interviews offer a real look at the experience.

*   High-signal content, not generic talks
*   Time to actually meet speakers and peers
*   A community that returns year after year

## About us

Artificial Intelligence (AI) is not a new field but recent advances in this space has generated many opportunities and raised many ethical issues. The [AI & Emerging Tech Meetup group](https://www.meetup.com/San-Francisco-AI-Meetup/) is spin-free, shill-free, platform-agnostic group that explores the latest developments and ethics of AI and other emerging technology spaces like blockchain, cryptocurrencies, NFTs, metaverse, and others.  
We host monthly events in San Francisco and New York. Our past speakers have included experts from Salesforce, Oracle, Microsoft, and other leading tech vendors in the AI space.   
**Also see:**

**Sign up for our weekly AI & Emerging Tech newsletter:**

**Code of Conduct:**  
Our events are sponsor-funded and volunteer-run. Our meetup groups and events are open to everyone regardless of their race, ethnicity, gender, orientation, or identity. We are emphatically trans-inclusive. We strive to accommodate all forms of disabilities as well as special dietary needs. Unless restricted by the venue policies, members are welcome to bring their children to our events if they are unable to find childcare.  
We reserve the right to refuse membership or entrance to anyone who is disruptive, dangerous, and/or poses a threat or harasses our members/speakers in any manner. Harassment includes but is not limited to heckling the members or speakers, purposeful misgendering, using any kind of slur, bullying, or any other attempt to intimidate other members or disrupt the events.  
We reserve the right to take action including but not limited to removing/blocking members who are in violation of our rules.  
There is no place in our group for hate or harassment.

[### AI meetup (SF) for Making Agentic AI Enterprise-Ready](/event/san-francisco-ai-meetup-sf-for-making-agentic-ai-enterprise-ready-may-2026)

Enterprise AI is evolving beyond simple chatbots and prompt engineering — the next frontier is \*\*agentic AI systems\*\* that can reason, plan, and operate autonomously within complex business environments. This [San Francisco](https://www.createwith.com/cities/san-francisco) meetup brings together startup founders, builders, and enterprise leaders to tackle one of the biggest unsolved challenges: how do we build AI agents that actually remember, learn, and scale in production? Hosted at the Capgemini Applied Innovation Exchange, this evening dives deep into the architectural and operational requirements for making agentic AI enterprise-ready. You'll hear from the teams building the frameworks and platforms that power the next generation of AI applications — from orchestration layers and memory systems to multi-agent coordination and retrieval architectures. ## What You'll Learn The event covers both strategic and technical perspectives on enterprise agentic AI: - \*\*Enterprise architecture patterns\*\* for deploying autonomous agent systems at scale - \*\*Memory and context management\*\* — how agents retain knowledge and learn over time - \*\*Multi-agent orchestration\*\* — coordinating teams of specialized AI agents to solve complex workflows - \*\*Real-world deployment challenges\*\* and lessons from teams shipping agent-based products today - \*\*The evolving AI application stack\*\* — new primitives, frameworks, and infrastructure for agentic systems ## Speaker Lineup You'll hear from founders and technical leaders at the forefront of the agentic AI movement: - \*\*Jerry Liu\*\*, Co-Founder and CEO of \*\*LlamaIndex\*\* — the leading data framework for LLM applications, powering retrieval and knowledge systems for thousands of AI products - \*\*João (Joe) Moura\*\*, Founder and CEO of \*\*CrewAI\*\* — a framework for orchestrating role-playing, autonomous AI agents that work together on complex tasks - \*\*Tara Khani\*\*, Co-Founder and CEO of \*\*Moorcheh.ai\*\* — building enterprise-grade agentic AI solutions - \*\*Majid Fekri\*\*, Co-Founder and CTO of \*\*Moorcheh.ai\*\* — deep technical expertise in AI agent architectures Each speaker will share insights from building and deploying agent systems in production, followed by Q&A and open discussion. ## Who Should Attend This meetup is ideal for: - \*\*AI engineers and developers\*\* working with agent frameworks, RAG systems, or LLM orchestration - \*\*Technical founders\*\* building products on top of agentic AI platforms - \*\*Enterprise architects

AI Council 2026 brings the frontier of artificial intelligence to [San Francisco](https://www.createwith.com/cities/san-francisco) for three intensive days of technical learning, hands-on experimentation, and deep networking. Running May 12-14, 2026 at the Marriott Marquis in SOMA, this vendor-neutral conference is designed for engineers, researchers, and builders who are actively shaping the future of AI. ## What Makes AI Council Different AI Council stands apart with its "No BS Guarantee" — every session is vendor-neutral, technically rigorous, and presented by practitioners doing real implementation work at companies like Anthropic, OpenAI, Meta, and Databricks. This isn't a marketing showcase; it's a community-driven event focused on sharing production learnings, architectural decisions, and hard-won insights from teams building AI systems at scale. ## Conference Structure The event features \*\*60+ talks\*\* distributed across \*\*10 specialized tracks\*\*: Inference Systems, AI Engineering, AI Security & Safety, Agent Infrastructure, Coding Agents & Autonomous Dev, Model Systems, Data Engineering & Databases, Applied AI, Analytics & Data Science, and Lightning Talks. This structure allows attendees to dive deep into their areas of focus while exposing them to adjacent domains that increasingly intersect in production AI deployments. Beyond the main stage, AI Council includes \*\*hands-on workshops\*\* led by tool creators, giving you direct experience with cutting-edge products and frameworks. After each talk, \*\*speaker office hours\*\* provide rare 1:1 access to presenters — an opportunity to explore technical details, discuss implementation challenges, or get feedback on your own projects. ## Who Should Attend This conference is ideal for software engineers building AI-powered applications, data scientists deploying models to production, ML engineers optimizing inference systems, platform engineers supporting AI infrastructure, and technical leaders evaluating AI strategies. The content assumes technical familiarity and focuses on real-world implementation rather than high-level theory. ## Networking & Community AI Council creates extensive opportunities for meaningful connections through conference happy hours and community-hosted after-hours events throughout the week. The single-track-within-themes format and speaker accessibility foster deeper conversations than typical large-scale conferences. As a member of NYC Data Engineering & Science meetups,

## What to Expect Designed for developers and platform teams navigating the challenging transition from AI prototypes to production systems, this technical meetup examines why most AI workflows fail to scale and what actually works in real-world deployments. While experiments and POCs may show promise, production environments demand robust workflows, governance, and trust mechanisms that many teams struggle to implement. ## Featured Tech Talk \*\*From AI Experiments to Production: How Teams Actually Scale AI Workflows\*\* will be presented by Rob Whiteley from Coder. This deep-dive session addresses the critical pain points facing development teams: experiments that work locally but break in production, AI-generated code that accelerates development but disrupts established workflows, autonomous agents that produce output teams can't trust or scale, and governance frameworks added too late that throttle velocity. The talk focuses on redesigning development workflows to work harmoniously with AI rather than simply layering new tools onto existing processes. You'll explore practical approaches to structuring workflows, managing autonomous agents, and introducing appropriate controls that maintain developer productivity. This isn't a tool comparison or hype session — it's a technical examination of production realities backed by real-world implementation experiences. ## Event Format The evening begins with registration and networking over food (5:30-6:00pm), followed by community updates and welcomes (6:00-6:15pm). The main tech talk and Q&A session runs from 6:15-7:30pm, providing ample time for deep technical discussion. The event concludes with an open discussion and networking mixer (7:30-8:30pm), giving you extended opportunities to connect with speakers and fellow developers tackling similar challenges. ## Who Should Attend This showcase is built for technical audiences — developers, ML engineers, platform engineers, DevOps professionals, and engineering leaders responsible for moving AI capabilities from experimentation to production. Whether you're struggling with agent reliability, workflow redesign, governance implementation, or production scaling challenges, you'll find actionable insights and peer connections. ## Community & Networking Co-hosted by AICamp and Workato, this series brings together two major developer communities for high-impact technical networking. Join AWS and leading industry partners at the AWS Loft in [San Francisco](https://www.createwith.com/cities/san-francisco) for an in-depth exploration of production-ready agentic AI systems. This fourth edition of the AWS Agentic AI Partner Showcase brings together technical experts from AWS, Coder, Mistral AI, Workato, Arize AI, and Moody's to demonstrate the complete development lifecycle—from initial code through deployment, monitoring, and validation. ## What Makes This Event Unique This isn't just another AI talk series. The showcase features live demonstrations of the complete development stack required to build, deploy, and maintain agentic AI systems in production environments. You'll see real implementations, not theoretical concepts, with practical insights into AWS integration patterns and production deployment strategies. The event combines main-stage technical presentations with interactive partner booths, allowing you to engage directly with solution providers throughout the evening. These industry trailblazers will share insider perspectives on where AI search is heading and what it means for brands. Second, Profound will present proprietary research derived from massive consumer data sets, offering actionable insights you won't find anywhere else. Third, you'll learn battle-tested playbooks from real Profound customers who are actively optimizing for AI search engines. ## Featured Speakers & Topics The speaker lineup represents the cutting edge of AI search expertise. \*\*Asad Awan\*\* from OpenAI will discuss ads and monetization strategies, while \*\*Jeff Titterton\*\* (Chief Marketing Officer at Stripe) and \*\*Reema Batta\*\* (VP of Growth Marketing at Figma) will share how leading tech companies are adapting to answer engines. \*\*Rob Gaige\*\*, Global Head of Insights at Reddit, brings unique perspectives on how platforms are responding to AI-driven discovery. \*\*Daniel Shin Un Kang\*\*, Head of Organic & Agentic Search at Expedia, will reveal how travel booking is transforming in the age of AI agents. Other notable speakers include \*\*Parag Agrawal\*\* (Founder of Parallel), \*\*Dave Steer\*\* (CMO of [Webflow](/tools/webflow)), and \*\*Mike King\*\* (Founder & CEO of iPullRank). The Profound team—including co-founders \*\*James Cadwallader\*\* (CEO) and \*\*Dylan Babbs\*\* (CTO), along with \*\*Nick Lafferty\*\* (Head of Growth Marketing) and \*\*Josh Blyskal\*\* (AEO Strategist)—will share the platform's latest innovations in answer engine analytics and optimization. ## Event Format & Networking Zero Click SF runs from 1:30 PM to

# June 25 - AI, ML and Computer Vision Meetup

1 attendee from 48 groups hosting

![Photo of Ai in Cloud Computing San Francisco group](https://secure.meetupstatic.com/photos/event/2/0/3/d/event_504068253.jpeg?w=3840)

### Ai in Cloud Computing San Francisco

## Details

Join our virtual meetup to hear talks from experts on cutting-edge topics across AI, ML, and computer vision.

**Date, Time and Location**

Jun 25, 2026  
9AM PST  
Online. **[Register for the Zoom!](https://voxel51.com/events/ai-ml-and-computer-vision-meetup-june-25-2026)**

Large-Scale Scene Reconstruction via Local View Transformers

Transformer-based models have advanced 3D scene reconstruction, but their quadratic attention limits scalability to large scenes. We introduce the Local View Transformer (LVT), which replaces global attention with locality-aware attention over neighboring views, conditioned on relative camera geometry. LVT decodes directly into 3D Gaussian splats with view-dependent color and opacity for high-fidelity rendering. Our approach enables scalable, single-pass reconstruction of large, high-resolution scenes.

*About the Speaker*

[Tooba Imtiaz](https://www.linkedin.com/in/tooba-imtiaz/) is a PhD candidate in Electrical and Computer Engineering at Northeastern University, working in the Machine Learning Lab. Her research focuses on 3D computer vision, novel view synthesis, and robust machine learning. She has published in top venues including SIGGRAPH Asia, CVPR, and ICLR, and has industry experience at Google.

**Lessons learned from running AI workloads in production**

He’ll share his “tales from the engine room” - practical insights from operating AI systems at scale, including the challenges of abstraction layers, the realities of data movement and hardware constraints, and how systems thinking is essential for building high-performance, secure, and responsible AI infrastructure.

*About the Speaker*

[Dave Hughes](https://www.linkedin.com/in/-davehughes-/) is CTO at Stelia. He was formerly CTO at Genesis Cloud, which pioneered what is now commonly known as 'neoclouds', and Principal Engineer/Interim Director of Engineering at Adjust GmbH where he built large-scale data warehousing and processing. Dave has a strong background in software engineering, data engineering, systems admin and network engineering. He has worked in traditional HPC, early GPU-accelerated computing (ML) and now AI.

**Enhancing Low-Field MRI with Deep Super-Resolution for Improved Nipah Virus Neuroimaging**

Advances in deep learning make very-low-field (VLF) MRI systems a viable alternative for in vivo neuroimaging. Zero-shot super-resolution, self-supervised learning, and generative AI were explored to improve the quality of low-field MRI images. We present a framework for the first deployment of a VLF scanner for imaging Nipah virus-inoculated nonhuman primates (NHPs) using a 0.05 T MRI system.

First, a retrospective simulation study assessed the feasibility of imaging NiV infection at low field, followed by a prospective deployment (0.05 T) that enabled longitudinal imaging. The VLF-NiV imaging was characterized by low image quality and included multiple contrasts. A deep learning-based unpaired domain adaptation (CycleGAN) conditioned on acquisition parameters was used to harmonize contrast, and a simulation-based ResUNet model was used to reduce unwanted noise and preserve T2-weighted structural fidelity. We also highlight studies involving zero-shot super-resolution and denoising experiments that are advantageous for accessible neuroimaging.

*About the Speaker*

[Ajay Sharma](https://www.linkedin.com/in/ajaysharma1996/) is a deep learning engineer with a broad background in biomedical image analysis. My research focuses on developing advanced deep learning methods for computer-aided disease detection and diagnosis. Currently, my work centers on improving image analysis in magnetic resonance imaging (MRI), with emphasis on low-field MRI (LF-MRI), image acquisition, image enhancement, brain tracking, segmentation, and reporting. Previously, I developed explainable AI (XAI) approaches for chest and pediatric brain imaging that increase clinicians’ confidence in AI-assisted diagnostic systems.

**And Now for Something Completely Different with FiftyOne**

Often the best way to understand what a tool is truly capable of, is to use in ways it was never intended to be used. This session pushes FiftyOne past its computer vision roots through a series of demos showing how to push the boundaries with FiftyOne. A few practical, some playful, all built with open source code. You'll see how FiftyOne's core building blocks generalize far beyond labeled datasets, and leave with patterns and ideas you can take in your own direction.

About the Speaker

[Burhan Qaddoumi](https://www.linkedin.com/in/burhan-qa/) is a ML DevRel Engineer at Voxel51 and perpetual "new guy" as a life long learner. Active in communities all across the web, eager to help, learn, and share with others that demonstrate initiative, interest, and drive.

## Experience the future ofenterprise data and intelligence

## 190+Partners

Join fellow data and AI leaders at Snowflake Summit 26 in San Francisco to lead your organization in this next era of agents and enterprise intelligence. See how to democratize connected agentic experiences across your organization. Discover how to deliver open, interoperable architectures without complexity. Learn from experts and peers on how to bring all your data together with universal governance. Gain the practical skills to build and deploy enterprise-ready agentic solutions that drive true business transformation.

## On site at Summit

![Sample alt text](https://www.snowflake.com/adobe/dynamicmedia/deliver/dm-aid--9187cbb3-f02d-4e26-bbfa-f89091da6fd1/onsite-at-summit-1.png?preferwebp=true&quality=85)

#### Basecamp Expo Hall

Collaborate on site with over 190 partner booths, explore the Industry Zone and Platform Peak, and dive into interactive Snowflake demos. Don’t miss out on more than 150 theater sessions covering a wide range of topics.

![Sample alt text](https://www.snowflake.com/adobe/dynamicmedia/deliver/dm-aid--d08703e3-7393-4db6-9e11-3377b8dfda4a/onsite-at-summit-2.png?preferwebp=true&quality=85)

#### Builders Hub

In the heart of Basecamp lies the Builders Hub — a developers’ oasis. Participate in theater and bootcamp sessions, engage with Snowflake’s Data Superheroes, win cool prizes and watch never-before-seen demos.

![Sample alt text](https://www.snowflake.com/adobe/dynamicmedia/deliver/dm-aid--cd0cc655-c83d-449c-ab4c-55210c2d5d7f/onsite-at-summit-3.png?preferwebp=true&quality=85)

#### Platform Peak

Bring your toughest product questions while meeting 1:1 with our expert team. Explore live demos of Snowflake in action and discover how to unlock endless possibilities with data, AI and application collaboration.

![summit industry](https://www.snowflake.com/adobe/dynamicmedia/deliver/dm-aid--ff974973-2d11-4be6-b2ca-3044da66cb25/onsite-snowflake-industry.jpg?preferwebp=true&quality=85)

#### Your industry at Summit

Join industry-specific sessions with data and AI leaders, explore the Vertical Village for live demos and insights from Snowflake experts, and connect with peers at happy hours and networking events.

## Event Pricing

#### FULL CONFERENCE PASS

##### WHAT’S INCLUDED

##### 30+ Full ConferenceRegister between January 13–June 4, 2026. ($600 off early bird / $800 off regular).$1,495

##### 100+ Full ConferenceRegister between January 13–June 4, 2026. $1,395

#### TRAINING ADD-ONS

Ready to dive deep and skill up with Snowflake? Summit is your chance to fast-track your Snowflake expertise through exclusive, expert-led training that expand your skills, deepen your knowledge and deliver real-world impact.

![](https://www.snowflake.com/adobe/dynamicmedia/deliver/dm-aid--3446c875-e0dc-40a3-9ca1-baca6afe662d/summit-training-beginner.png?preferwebp=true&quality=85)

#### GenAI Essentials

*Only at Summit.*

Turn your enterprise data into intelligent, AI-powered results. This immersive, full day training is designed for technical practitioners ready to move GenAI from concept to production. You will get hands-on experience leveraging GenAI in Snowflake, including Snowflake Cortex, and Snowflake Intelligence to tackle real-world cases.

Your training pass also includes a SnowPro Specialty: GenAI exam voucher (valued at $225) which can be used anytime 6 months after Summit.

Review the Course Datasheet [here](https://www.snowflake.com/content/dam/snowflake-site/summit/standard_genai_essentials_datasheet_26C25.pdf).

Available Day 1 only.

### $1000

*Walk-in pricing: $1,200*

![](https://www.snowflake.com/adobe/dynamicmedia/deliver/dm-aid--5721eb5b-d97d-492c-a11f-5ccae066a011/summit-training-advanced.png?preferwebp=true&quality=85)

#### Snowflake Accelerator

*Only at Summit.*

Jumpstart your productivity with Snowflake in just one day. This curated, hands-on accelerator course is designed for technical practitioners new to the platform or seeking to master core features for maximum efficiency. You will skip the ramp-up and immediately leverage Snowflake's most powerful features.  You’ll leave ready to build, query and operate efficiently from here forward.

Your training pass includes a SnowPro Core exam voucher (valued at $175) which can be used anytime 6 months after Summit.

Review the Course Datasheet [here](https://www.snowflake.com/content/dam/snowflake-site/summit/standard_snowflake_accelerator_datasheet_26C25.pdf).

Available Day 1 only.

### $700

*Walk-in pricing: $800*

![Attendees collaborating at Snowflake Summit 25](https://www.snowflake.com/adobe/dynamicmedia/deliver/dm-aid--ac8f35de-1e02-4ceb-b758-983c5b0d8911/summit-training.jpg?preferwebp=true&quality=85)

#### Snowflake Platform Training

Designed for individuals with limited or no prior Snowflake experience, this half-day course provides an essential, hands-on walkthrough of Snowflake. Through intensive interactive labs, you will learn how to unify, integrate and share previously siloed data with virtually unlimited scalability and concurrency.

Plus, get hands-on with Cortex Code, Snowflake's built-in AI agent. You'll collaborate with AI to build a personalized, interactive application — shaped by what you've covered in this course — that you can take away and use to keep learning.

Review the Course Datasheet [here](https://www.snowflake.com/content/dam/snowflake-site/summit/standard_spt_datasheet.pdf).

Available June 1, June 3 and June 4.

### $100

*Walk-in pricing: $200*

# AI Engineer World’s Fair 2026

## The World’s Largest Technical Conference for AI Engineers

[**IN-PERSON**](https://www.ai.engineer/worldsfair) **| June 30–July 2, 2026 | Moscone Center | San Francisco, CA, USA**

AI Engineer World’s Fair 2026 is the world’s largest technical conference dedicated to building, deploying, and scaling artificial intelligence systems. Hosted at Moscone Center in San Francisco, this premier event brings together AI engineers, founders, researchers, and senior AI leaders to explore the cutting edge of applied artificial intelligence.

The conference features an expansive technical program with over 20 tracks, 250 speakers, and 100+ expo partners, attracting more than 6,000 AI engineers, founders, and VPs of AI from around the globe. Attendees will engage in deep technical learning, hands-on demonstrations, and peer-to-peer collaboration focused on real-world AI implementation. Key highlights include:

* **Technical Tracks & Expert-Led Sessions** – Dive deep into applied AI, machine learning infrastructure, LLMs, MLOps, and production-scale systems.
* **Industry & Startup Expo** – Explore tools, platforms, and technologies from leading AI companies and emerging innovators.
* **Speakers & Thought Leadership** – Hear from top practitioners and leaders shaping the future of artificial intelligence.
* **Networking & Community Engagement** – Connect with AI engineers, founders, and executives advancing AI across industries.

AI Engineer World’s Fair 2026 is where the global AI engineering community comes together to shape the future of intelligent systems. Mark your calendar and join us at Moscone Center in San Francisco.

## HEAR FROM LEADERS AT THE WORLD'S TOP AI AND TECH COMPANIES.

![Microsoft](/_next/static/media/microsoft-wf26.1e63805e.svg?dpl=dpl_4SU9QruxpWeBrHdJkHvkXRCVjLf8)![AWS](/_next/static/media/aws-wf26.709eea9c.svg?dpl=dpl_4SU9QruxpWeBrHdJkHvkXRCVjLf8)![Anthropic](/_next/static/media/anthropic-wf26.51bedfb1.svg?dpl=dpl_4SU9QruxpWeBrHdJkHvkXRCVjLf8)![Google DeepMind](/_next/static/media/google-deepmind-wf26.c215b97d.svg?dpl=dpl_4SU9QruxpWeBrHdJkHvkXRCVjLf8)![OpenAI](/_next/static/media/openai-wf26.b0a8978e.svg?dpl=dpl_4SU9QruxpWeBrHdJkHvkXRCVjLf8)![Microsoft](/_next/static/media/microsoft-wf26.1e63805e.svg?dpl=dpl_4SU9QruxpWeBrHdJkHvkXRCVjLf8)![AWS](/_next/static/media/aws-wf26.709eea9c.svg?dpl=dpl_4SU9QruxpWeBrHdJkHvkXRCVjLf8)![Anthropic](/_next/static/media/anthropic-wf26.51bedfb1.svg?dpl=dpl_4SU9QruxpWeBrHdJkHvkXRCVjLf8)![Google DeepMind](/_next/static/media/google-deepmind-wf26.c215b97d.svg?dpl=dpl_4SU9QruxpWeBrHdJkHvkXRCVjLf8)![OpenAI](/_next/static/media/openai-wf26.b0a8978e.svg?dpl=dpl_4SU9QruxpWeBrHdJkHvkXRCVjLf8)

AI Engineer runs the most viewed technical conferences in AI for engineers, with over 10M+ views of our talks online. We are back in SF for the 4th year in a row!

This is the one place you can meet with every major frontier lab, leading AI clouds, and AI native/transformed companies — from disruptive AI startups to Fortune 500 AI leaders, and every notable building block in the LLM OS ecosystem.

### KEY DATES FOR SPEAKERS

April 12First Draft Submission Deadline

mid-AprilFirst Wave Speaker Announcements

May 30ALL Submissions Deadline

June 2Final Wave Announcements

All submissions are reviewed using the same criteria; deadlines only affect review timing and announcement date.

### SUBMIT YOUR PROPOSAL

We invite you to submit your abstract and speaker information. You are highly encouraged to:

* Pick non-boring titles (you CAN change your mind later) and look at the best talks from past AIE confs and peer conferences like JSConf, GDC, Goto, and StrangeLoop. Your chances are ~epsilon if you simply resubmit talks that look at home at generic AI conferences.
* Multiple submissions are welcome and not held against you — you don't need to guess at what we want. We just need to know the rough direction of 1–5 topics you could speak about.

### TALK LENGTHS

Workshop

1–2 hours • Hands-on sessions or technical deep dives

Stage Talk

15–20 mins • Keynotes, live demos, paper presentations

Lightning Talk

5–10 mins • Launches, hot takes, fun rants

Online Talk

5–55 mins • Very free form

During your session, dive right into the content with minimal self-introduction — the MC will introduce you. Your talk will be professionally recorded and published on our YouTube, X, and LinkedIn.

[SUBMIT YOUR PROPOSAL](https://app.ai.engineer/e/ai-engineer-worlds-fair-2026/speaker-registration)

WHAT TO EXPECT

## THE AI ENGINEER WORLD'S FAIR IS THE LARGEST INDUSTRY EVENT TO DISCOVER WHAT'S NOW AND ENGINEER THE FUTURE OF AI

![](/highlight-image-1.jpg)

### TALKS FROM ENGINEERS WHO SHIP

No theoretical promissory hoopla. Just engineers and founders on the cutting edge of AI Engineering, sharing their knowledge.

![](/highlight-image-3.jpg)

### AI LEADERSHIP TRACKS

Exclusive tracks for CTOs and VPs of AI on running scaled AI orgs and enterprise transformations. Closed-door discussions, sit-down meals, and VIP networking with the most senior AI leaders in the industry.

![](/highlight-image-2.jpg)

### IN-DEPTH WORKSHOPS

We've curated over 50+ workshops for you to choose from, servicing everyone from beginners to experienced AI engineers.

![ACM CAIS](/_next/static/media/cais-logo.4a783f5d.svg?dpl=dpl_4SU9QruxpWeBrHdJkHvkXRCVjLf8)

### POSTER SESSIONS & INDUSTRY PAPERS

New for 2026! Featuring top industry papers in partnership with ACM CAIS 2026. Peer review submissions due Feb 27.

[Submit your paper →](https://www.caisconf.org/pages/cfp/)

![](/highlight-image-4.jpg)

### THE NERDIEST FUN YOU CAN HAVE!

Stimulation for your brain, inspiration for your soul, education for your business.

29 TRACKS • 400+ SESSIONS
